goal: Enumerate the data work needed to expand the calculator to broad U.S. city coverage while preserving factual accuracy, provenance, and auditability.

fixed_user_profile.housing_profile: renter living alone in a one-bedroom apartment

source_policy.primary_rule: Use official government sources for geography, housing, price indexes, inflation, and spending data wherever possible. Use PolicyEngine US for tax modeling.
source_policy.manual_entry_policy: Manual overrides are allowed only as reviewed exceptions with source citation, reviewer, date, and reason.
forbidden_as_core_sources: MIT Living Wage Calculator scraped data
allowed_as_validation_only: Public calculator outputs from Forbes, NerdWallet, Bankrate, or similar tools may be used only as qualitative comparison checks, not as source data.

global_acceptance_criteria: Every generated city record has stable identifiers for Census place, state, county mapping, metro mapping, housing source geography, tax geography, and price-index geography.
Every numeric field has source name, vintage, source row/key, ingestion timestamp, transform version, and checksum or reproducible query reference.
No city-level value is silently hand-entered into application code.
All high-impact ambiguous geography mappings are explicitly flagged with confidence and explanation.
The calculator can lazily load a city-level data file without requiring all cities to load in the browser.

A reverse-check test exists: source A to target B, then target B equivalent salary back to source A should approximately recover the original salary, subject to interpolation and income-band boundaries.
Spot-check reports exist for at least one city per state plus all special local-tax jurisdictions.

task_groups.name: Scope and Data Contract
task_groups.purpose: Freeze definitions before gathering any nationwide data.

tasks.title: Define city universe rule
tasks.agent_brief: Define exactly which U.S. places are in scope. Do not assume the final count. Produce a rule, then later the data will determine the count.
inputs_needed: Product requirement: broad U.S. city coverage
Population threshold decision, e.g. incorporated places and census-designated places above a chosen population threshold
outputs: machine-readable inclusion rule in nationwide data config
acceptance_checks: Rule says whether to include incorporated places, census-designated places, county equivalents, Puerto Rico, and other territories.
Rule says whether cities below the population threshold can be manually included.
Rule avoids hard-coding an assumed number such as 300.

tasks.title: Define canonical data schema
tasks.agent_brief: Design the city, geography, housing, price, spending, tax-curve, and provenance schema before ingestion.
outputs: src/lib/nationwideTypes.ts draft or JSON schema draft
acceptance_checks: Schema distinguishes display city from tax county, HUD rent geography, and BEA price geography.
Schema includes confidence fields and provenance fields.
Schema supports one file per city for lazy loading.

tasks.title: Define review gates
tasks.agent_brief: Create a checklist that every data source and transform must pass before it can enter generated app data.
acceptance_checks: Checklist includes source vintage, license/redistribution check, checksum, row counts, missing values, and spot checks.
Checklist defines when a city gets high, medium, or low confidence.

task_groups.name: Official Geography and City Universe
task_groups.purpose: Build the canonical national place list and map each place to counties and metro areas.

tasks.title: Ingest Census place universe
primary_sources: U.S. Census TIGER/Line or TIGERweb place data
acceptance_checks: Every included place has a Census GEOID.
No duplicate display city/state pairs without disambiguation.
Population threshold and inclusion rule are applied reproducibly.

tasks.title: Build place-to-county mapping
acceptance_checks: Multi-county places are detected, not flattened silently.
Each mapping records all intersecting counties and the selected computation county or weighting rule.
High-impact multi-county cities are flagged for review.
review_notes: The initial local implementation chose one county. Nationwide coverage should explicitly decide whether to keep one computation county or use population-weighted counties where tax modeling supports it.

tasks.title: Build place-to-CBSA mapping
primary_sources: Office of Management and Budget CBSA delineation files
acceptance_checks: Each city has a CBSA where applicable.
Non-CBSA or ambiguous cases are flagged with fallback policy.
CBSA code and name match the BEA RPP geography.

tasks.title: Identify special local-tax jurisdictions
primary_sources: Official city/state/local tax agency pages for validation
acceptance_checks: Cities with known local income/payroll taxes are explicitly listed.
Each local tax is marked as modeled by PolicyEngine, unsupported, or needs custom official-source implementation.
Unsupported local taxes produce a visible confidence warning rather than silent omission.

task_groups.name: Housing Cost Data
task_groups.purpose: Map each city to an official one-bedroom rent measure.

tasks.title: Ingest HUD Fair Market Rent data
primary_sources: HUD USER Fair Market Rent datasets
acceptance_checks: Data is ingested from a tabular official HUD source where available, not manually extracted from PDFs for nationwide scale.
One-bedroom FMR exists for every mapped HUD area.
Monthly and annual rent values are both generated, with annual = monthly * 12.

tasks.title: Map cities to HUD FMR areas
acceptance_checks: Every city maps to exactly one chosen HUD FMR area or is flagged for review.
Metro-area and county-level FMR differences are resolved with an explicit rule.
Ambiguous mappings include explanation and confidence.

tasks.title: Optional housing validation against ACS
primary_sources: U.S. Census ACS gross rent tables
acceptance_checks: Validation is not used to override HUD FMR unless a separate reviewed method is approved.
Large HUD-vs-ACS divergences are flagged for human review.

task_groups.name: Non-Housing Spending and Price Data
task_groups.purpose: Build the national spending basket and reprice it to each metro.

tasks.title: Ingest BEA Regional Price Parities
primary_sources: BEA Regional Price Parities MARPP data
acceptance_checks: All CBSA codes used by city mappings have BEA values or documented fallback.
Line codes are recorded and validated.
Non-CBSA fallback policy is documented before generation.

tasks.title: Build BLS CEX national spending bands
primary_sources: BLS Consumer Expenditure Survey Public Use Microdata
acceptance_checks: Filtering rule exactly matches fixed profile: single-person renter consumer units with wage/salary income.
Income bands are documented and CPI-adjusted.
Unweighted and weighted record counts are reported for each band.
Sparse bands are flagged.

tasks.title: Ingest BLS CPI inflation factor
primary_sources: BLS CPI-U all items, U.S. city average, not seasonally adjusted
acceptance_checks: Base CEX year and target month are explicit.
Inflation factor is reproducible from source index values.

tasks.title: Review category-to-price-index weights
acceptance_checks: Each non-housing category has an explicit BEA price-index weighting.
Weights sum to 1 for every category.
Any heuristic weighting is labeled as methodological assumption, not source fact.

task_groups.name: Tax Curves
task_groups.purpose: Generate gross-to-net tax curves for every supported city.

tasks.title: Audit PolicyEngine city/county/state parameter support
acceptance_checks: State income tax support is verified for all states in scope.
Local income/payroll tax support is verified or flagged.
Unsupported jurisdictions are listed before tax curves are generated.

tasks.title: Generate salary grid specification
acceptance_checks: Grid covers all salaries the UI supports.
Interpolation error tolerance is defined.
Out-of-range behavior is documented.

tasks.title: Generate city tax curves
acceptance_checks: Each city curve records PolicyEngine version, tax year, state, county, and local flags.
Curves include component arrays for federal income tax, payroll tax, state tax, local tax, state payroll items, residual, total tax, and net income.
Curve monotonicity checks pass or exceptions are reviewed.

task_groups.name: Integrated City Data Generation
task_groups.purpose: Join geography, housing, price, spending, and tax data into app-ready city files.

tasks.title: Build nationwide city index
acceptance_checks: Index contains lightweight city metadata only.
Large tax curves remain in lazy-loaded city files.
Every city has confidence and resolution notes.

tasks.title: Build per-city lazy-load files
acceptance_checks: Each file includes only the data needed for that city.
Imports are deterministic and generated from a manifest.
Bundle size is measured before and after generation.

tasks.title: Build source/provenance manifest
acceptance_checks: Manifest lists every source file/API endpoint, vintage, checksum, retrieval time, and transform script.
Manifest can be displayed in the Sources page without manual copy-paste.

task_groups.name: Quality Assurance and Review
task_groups.purpose: Catch factual errors before generated data is shipped.

tasks.title: Run structural QA
checks: All tax curves have same grid length
All annual rent values equal monthly rent times 12

tasks.title: Run numerical QA
checks: Tax curve net income equals gross minus total tax within tolerance
Tax curve net income is broadly non-decreasing
Cost basket equals sum of components

tasks.title: Human spot-check packet
acceptance_checks: At least one city per state is included.
All cities with special local tax flags are included.
Each spot check has direct source citations and computed outputs.

tasks.title: Regression checks against baseline city outputs
acceptance_checks: Austin, Sunnyvale, New York, Seattle, Denver, and Raleigh remain explainable.
Any changed value is attributed to a source-vintage or methodology change.

task_groups.name: Agent Handoff Strategy
task_groups.purpose: Split the work into small independent assignments.
task_groups.handoff_rule: No agent should both gather a source dataset and approve its final app integration. A separate QA pass must review source rows, transformations, and generated outputs.
